content.content:
The elastic user password must be rotated.

OBSERVED STATE:
- Secret elastic-password contains the current password.
- Secret elastic-password-next contains the new password.
- An auth-checker deployment is expected to stay healthy.

YOUR TASK:
- Rotate the elastic user password to match elastic-password-next.
- Update the elastic-password secret to the new value.
- Ensure auth-checker becomes healthy after the rotation.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Do not install CRDs or use operators.
- Avoid prolonged downtime.

GOAL:
- Old password fails, new password succeeds.
- auth-checker is Ready.---ENV---
KARMA_KUBECTL_PROXY_PORT=42003
KUBECONFIG=/mydata/karma/runs/short-rerun/elasticsearch-full-lifecycle-20260618_105654/bundle/kubeconfig
---
agent.log
precondition.log
prompt.txt
proxy-client.crt
proxy-client.key
proxy.pid

echo "=== next pw ==="; kubectl -n $ns get secret elastic-password-next -o jsonpath='{.data}'; echo
echo "=== auth-checker deploy ==="; kubectl -n $ns get deploy auth-checker -o yaml 2>&1 | sed -n '1,120p'

kubectl -n $ns exec es-cluster-0 -- curl -s --max-time 8 -u "elastic:elasticpass-elastic-password-next" http://localhost:9200/_security/_authenticate; echo

input.command:
ns=elasticsearch
kubectl -n $ns exec es-cluster-0 -- sh -c 'cat /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml 2>/dev/null' 2>&1 | grep -iE "ssl|http|security"

content.content:
cluster.name: es-cluster
node.name: ${POD_NAME}
network.host: 0.0.0.0
cluster.initial_master_nodes:
  - es-cluster-0
  - es-cluster-1
  - es-cluster-2
discovery.seed_hosts:
  - es-cluster-0.es-cluster
  - es-cluster-1.es-cluster
  - es-cluster-2.es-cluster
node.roles: [ master, data, ingest ]
node.store.allow_mmap: false
xpack.security.enabled: true
xpack.security.http.ssl:
  enabled: true
  key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
  certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
  certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
xpack.security.transport.ssl:
  enabled: true
  verification_mode: certificate
  key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
  certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
  certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt

content.text: ES uses HTTPS on 9200, but the auth-checker probe uses `http://`. Let me first do the core rotation, then address auth-checker readiness.

input.command:
ns=elasticsearch
echo "=== rotate elastic password via security API (auth with OLD) ==="
kubectl -n $ns exec es-cluster-0 -- curl -sk --max-time 10 -u "elastic:ElasticPass123!" \
  -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  https://localhost:9200/_security/user/elastic/_password \
  -d '{"password":"elasticpass-elastic-password-next"}'; echo
echo "=== verify new pw works ==="
kubectl -n $ns exec es-cluster-0 -- curl -sk --max-time 8 -u "elastic:elasticpass-elastic-password-next" https://localhost:9200/_security/_authenticate; echo
echo "=== verify old pw fails ==="
kubectl -n $ns exec es-cluster-0 -- curl -sk --max-time 8 -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" -u "elastic:ElasticPass123!" https://localhost:9200/_security/_authenticate; echo

content.content:

content.text: Password rotated. Now update the `elastic-password` secret to the new value.

input.command:
ns=elasticsearch
newb64=$(echo ZWxhc3RpY3Bhc3MtZWxhc3RpYy1wYXNzd29yZC1uZXh0)
kubectl -n $ns patch secret elastic-password --type merge -p "{\"data\":{\"password\":\"$newb64\"}}"
echo "=== verify secret ==="
kubectl -n $ns get secret elastic-password -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d; echo

content.text: Now restart auth-checker to pick up the new secret, and observe readiness.

input.command:
ns=elasticsearch
kubectl -n $ns rollout restart deploy/auth-checker
sleep 25
kubectl -n $ns get deploy auth-checker
kubectl -n $ns get pods -l app=auth-checker
